skill.name: react-zustand-patterns
skill.description: Guides React and React Native developers through practical Zustand store patterns, async action handling, selector stability, persistence, testing, debugging, and 5.x migration notes.
skill.summary: Apply reliable Zustand patterns for React state timing, async actions, selectors, persistence, tests, and migration work.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, storage, weak cryptography, and combined-risk heuristics. Review found these were false positives from Markdown backticks, TypeScript examples, API placeholder names, and explanatory Zustand persistence guidance. No prompt injection, malicious intent, credential access, executable script, or real outbound network behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The referenced lines use Markdown inline code or fenced TypeScript examples for Zustand APIs such as set, get, and getState, not Ruby backticks or executable shell calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is instructional Markdown and the cited blocks are TypeScript examples. There is no command runner, shell invocation, Ruby code, or instruction to execute arbitrary commands.

low_findings.title: API Fetch Examples Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: The network findings are false positives. The cited api.fetch calls are illustrative async action examples inside a Zustand store pattern and do not define a real endpoint or perform network access by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The calls are contained in a code sample that teaches awaitable async actions. No URL, credential, request body, or executable support file is present.

low_findings.title: Persistence Example Is Benign Browser Storage Guidance
low_findings.description: The browser storage finding is a false positive. The referenced section explains Zustand persist middleware and advises partial persistence of preferences, not reading secrets or exfiltrating stored data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a comment in an educational example and the sample limits persistence to preferences. No credential access or outbound transfer is shown.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Were False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are false positives. The cited lines contain frontmatter text, explanatory prose, and test descriptions, with no cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive hashing logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA1, cipher setup, password hashing, or token generation on these lines. The matches appear to come from generic substring heuristics.

low_findings.title: Combined Critical Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The critical obfuscation heuristic is false positive. The file combines Markdown code examples for state updates, async fetching, and local persistence, but contains no obfuscation, credential access, or executable delivery path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static ingredients are present only as instructional examples. Semantic review found no suspicious control text, encoded payloads, credential reads, or hidden execution chain.

content.value_statement: React state bugs often come from stale closures, batched renders, and async timing. This skill guides Zustand store design, debugging, testing, persistence, and migration decisions.
actual_capabilities: Explains how synchronous Zustand store updates relate to batched React renders.
Shows how to avoid stale closures with get and getState after async boundaries.
Guides stable selector usage with shallow comparison and Zustand 5.x useShallow.
Covers derived state, subscriptions, debugging middleware, and manual state logging.
Provides testing patterns for resetting stores and validating async store actions.
Describes persist middleware usage for browser preference storage.
limitations: It is a guidance-only skill and does not include runnable scripts or automation.
Examples use placeholder API calls and must be adapted to real application services.
It does not replace project-specific architecture review for large state models.
It focuses on Zustand patterns, not general Redux, MobX, or server cache strategy.

use_cases.title: Fix Async State Bugs
use_cases.description: Diagnose stale data after await calls and restructure store actions to read current Zustand state.

use_cases.title: Improve Store Performance
use_cases.description: Refine selectors, derived values, and shallow comparison choices to reduce avoidable component re-renders.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Patterns
use_cases.description: Create shared conventions for testing, persistence, debugging, and Zustand 5.x migration work.

prompt_templates.title: Explain My Store Timing
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a Zustand action.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how Zustand set, get, and React re-render timing apply to this store action. Identify any stale state risks.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Async Actions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Zustand async actions. Make them awaitable, handle loading and errors clearly, and re-read state after await points.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Selectors
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these Zustand selectors for avoidable re-renders. Recommend multiple selectors, shallow comparison, or useShallow where appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Store Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration checklist for this Zustand store from 4.x to 5.x. Include selector changes, type inference checks, tests, and risk areas.

output_examples.input: A store action sets loading, awaits a request, then uses a captured data value.
output_examples.output: The response flags the captured value as stale after the await and recommends reading current state before writing the result.

output_examples.input: A component selector returns a new object with several fields on every render.
output_examples.output: The response explains why the selector can trigger extra renders and suggests separate selectors or shallow comparison.

output_examples.input: A team wants to persist user preferences with Zustand middleware.
output_examples.output: The response recommends limiting persisted fields to preferences and avoiding sensitive session data.

best_practices: Read current state with get or getState after every async boundary.
Keep derived values computed from source state unless storing them is required.
Persist only the smallest safe state slice needed across sessions.
anti_patterns: Do not assume React components see updated Zustand values immediately after set.
Do not return new selector objects without shallow comparison or useShallow.
Do not persist tokens, secrets, or sensitive user data in browser storage.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guidance skill with TypeScript examples and no runnable scripts.

faq.question: Can it help with React Native?
faq.answer: Yes. The Zustand timing, selector, async action, and testing patterns apply to React Native stores.

faq.question: Does it cover Zustand 5.x?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes migration notes for shallow imports, useShallow, and improved type inference.

faq.question: Does it replace application testing?
faq.answer: No. It suggests store testing patterns, but teams still need project-specific tests.

faq.question: Is browser persistence always safe?
faq.answer: No. Persist only non-sensitive state such as preferences and avoid secrets or session tokens.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
